your_book:
Happy Fourth — the book had a strong holiday session. With a $20,697.98 account carrying $158.01 in unrealized gains (+0.77%), most of the heavy lifting came from the healthcare and software names. ADBE led the large positions, up 4.14% on the day ($3,442.27 value), though it remains underwater on a cost basis at −10.4% — a persistent gap between today's momentum and the entry thesis. ABT (+3.49%, $2,766.60) and ZTS (+3.24%, $2,468.40) were the standout quality performers; both carry Strong Buy verdicts and top-tier QARP scores (80.8 and 84.6 respectively), so price and thesis are aligned there. BSX popped 4.83% but is still −5.7% overall with an elevated guidance-cut flag — that bounce doesn't resolve the underlying problem. The only red ink on the day was NVDA (−1.39%), though it remains +6.2% on cost.

The drift stories that warrant honest scrutiny: LULU (+1.74% today, but −7.4% overall) is accumulating losses against a BUY verdict while Americas comps are in a fifth straight decline and margins are tariff-squeezed — price and thesis are visibly diverging. RKLB (−4.8% overall, AVOID verdict, QARP 37.0) and IOT (AVOID, QARP 48.5) both carry the book's weakest quality scores; IOT's +5.12% day is a distraction from the fact that neither name belongs in a capital-preservation mandate. ADBE's −10.4% total loss against a Strong Buy verdict is the largest dollar-weighted thesis-vs-price gap in the book.

Watch list heading into next week: BSX reports around July 23 with flat-through-Q3 guidance already on record — that earnings print will either validate or further erode the STRONG BUY case. NOW also reports ~July 23; at 14.4% of the book it's the single biggest concentration risk in a hawkish-rate environment. ABT reports ~July 17 with a trimmed EPS outlook already flagged. And keep an eye on KO — unusual options activity (2,061 Jul 17 $83 calls swept near the ask vs. just 362 open interest) ahead of its July 28 earnings is worth noting, even in a 0.8% position.

attention.headline: AVOID-rated, weakest QARP in book, still bleeding
attention.note: At QARP 37.0 and an AVOID verdict, RKLB is −4.8% overall and has no quality anchor — it's a 6.3% weight that is structurally misaligned with a capital-preservation mandate and needs a clear exit rationale or a position-closure decision.

attention.headline: Loss deepening while fundamentals deteriorate
attention.note: Five straight quarters of negative Americas comps, tariff-gutted margins, China stalled, and a guidance cut — yet the book still holds a BUY verdict against a −7.4% total loss, and the next earnings catalyst isn't until ~September 4, leaving the position exposed with no near-term reset.

attention.headline: Guidance slashed; earnings ~Jul 23 is a live risk event
attention.note: Today's 4.83% pop looks like relief-buying against a backdrop of FY26 growth cut from 10.5% to 7–8.5% and management guiding flat through Q3 — the July 23 print will determine whether STRONG BUY holds or needs to be revisited.

attention.headline: AVOID verdict, QARP 48.5 — today's gain doesn't change the picture
attention.note: Samsara's +5.12% day is the book's best single-session performer, but with an AVOID verdict and a below-threshold QARP of 48.5, the position's presence in a capital-preservation portfolio deserves a justification check before it grows any further on price alone.
